text.title: handoff-id-payload Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for typed handoffs and a shared store.

rule.statement: Handoff payloads MUST be `{task_id: str, target_agent: str, decision_metadata: object}`; they MUST NOT contain raw user input, conversation history, or prior agent's reasoning.
rule.rationale: Forwarding the conversation scales O(n²) in chain length. Carrying only an ID keeps every hop fixed-cost regardless of pipeline depth.

rule.statement: The receiving agent's FIRST action MUST be `store.get(task_id)` to load task state; the payload's `decision_metadata` is for routing decisions only, not for reconstructing inputs.
rule.rationale: If metadata and store disagree, the store wins. Duplicating state across both creates drift.

rule.statement: Supervisor routing decisions MUST be emitted as a `SupervisorDecision{target_agent, reason, decision_metadata}` structured object; free-form messages naming the next worker are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Free-form routing is fragile (model picks a worker name that does not exist); structured output makes routing a typed dispatch.

rule.statement: State updates MUST be append-only events; overwriting prior fields is forbidden so a downstream agent or re-run can replay history.
rule.rationale: Append-only logs let failed handoffs be retried without losing prior work; overwrites destroy evidence on retry.

rule.statement: The store MUST expose at minimum: `get(task_id) -> state`, `append_event(task_id, event)`, `set_status(task_id, status)`; no other ops are required and additional ops should be added sparingly.
rule.rationale: Three primitives cover the common cases; adding bespoke ops bloats the agent integration and ties the store to specific use cases.
